notes: Seeded from define-tech-design evals cases #7 (stage-3-gate-enforced) and #8 (stage-3-detects-uncovered-tr).
Added cases for new behavior unique to this split skill: filing component issues + gap issues, and refusing to inline per-component detail.

evals.name: gate-refuses-proposed-adrs
evals.prompt: We have three ADRs in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/: 0001 (accepted), 0002 (proposed), 0003 (proposed). Compose tech-design.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to compose because not every ADR is accepted. Should list the proposed ADRs (0002, 0003) and ask the user to accept them via define-adr or revise them. Should not write tech-design.md and should not file any issues.

assertions.name: refuses_to_compose
assertions.check: Response declines to write tech-design.md.

assertions.name: lists_proposed_adrs
assertions.check: Response specifically names ADR-0002 and ADR-0003 as the blocking proposed ADRs.

assertions.name: routes_to_define_adr
assertions.check: Response tells the user to accept the proposed ADRs (via define-adr) before re-invoking.

assertions.name: no_tech_design_written
assertions.check: No tech-design.md file is written under docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/.

assertions.name: no_issues_filed
assertions.check: No `gh issue create` invocations are made.

evals.name: detects-uncovered-tr
evals.prompt: All ADRs in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/ are accepted. tech-requirements.md has TR-01 through TR-08. Compose tech-design.md. (Note: TR-08 is 'graceful degradation when a GCP region is unreachable' and no ADR addresses it.)
evals.expected_output: While building the audit trail, should detect that TR-08 has no ADR. Should NOT paper over the gap with prose. Should stop, surface the gap explicitly, and propose returning to plan-adrs / define-adr to draft an ADR addressing TR-08 (or removing TR-08 from tech-requirements.md if the constraint isn't real).

assertions.name: detects_TR_08_uncovered
assertions.check: Response specifically calls out TR-08 as having no ADR addressing it.

assertions.name: does_not_paper_over_gap
assertions.check: Response does not invent a story / component / mechanism to satisfy TR-08 in tech-design.md prose.

assertions.name: routes_to_amend_adrs_or_trs
assertions.check: Response proposes either filing/amending an ADR for TR-08 (via plan-adrs / define-adr) or removing TR-08 from tech-requirements.md if the constraint is no longer real.

assertions.name: no_premature_completion
assertions.check: Skill does not declare tech-design.md complete or proceed to file component issues against an incomplete design.

evals.name: files-component-and-gap-issues
evals.prompt: All ADRs in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/ are accepted (0001 through 0003). tech-requirements.md has TR-01 through TR-07, all covered. Compose tech-design.md and file the follow-up issues — go.
evals.expected_output: Should walk the ADRs, identify components, surface any implementation-detail gaps (e.g. tenant ID format derivation, API contracts not yet specified, table schemas not yet defined), and after explicit approval file one component issue per component (titled `story(component): ...`) and one gap issue per gap (titled `story(gap): ...`). tech-design.md should contain a skeleton (overview + component inventory + audit trail) but NOT inline per-component detail like endpoint paths, table columns, or sequence diagrams.

assertions.name: writes_skeleton_tech_design
assertions.check: tech-design.md is produced with overview, component inventory, and TR → ADR → component audit trail.

assertions.name: no_inline_per_component_detail
assertions.check: tech-design.md does NOT contain endpoint paths, table column definitions, or per-UX sequence diagrams. Per-component detail is deferred to component design docs.

assertions.name: audit_trail_covers_all_TRs
assertions.check: Every TR-NN appears in the audit trail; no row has an empty ADR or Realization column.

assertions.name: files_one_issue_per_component
assertions.check: One `gh issue create` invocation per component, with title `story(component): {name} — {capability-name}` (or close-to-this convention).

assertions.name: component_issue_bodies_reference_define_component_design
assertions.check: Each component issue body names `define-component-design` as the next-step skill.

assertions.name: files_gap_issues_if_gaps_present
assertions.check: If gaps were surfaced, one `gh issue create` invocation per gap titled `story(gap): {name} — {capability-name}` (or close-to-this convention).

assertions.name: states_tech_design_not_complete_until_gaps_resolved
assertions.check: Response explicitly states tech design is not complete until all gap issues are resolved.

evals.name: refuses-to-write-per-component-detail-inline
evals.prompt: All ADRs are accepted in docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/adrs/. Compose tech-design.md and while you're at it write out the tenant-registry service's API endpoints — I know what they should be: GET /tenants, POST /tenants, GET /tenants/{id}, PATCH /tenants/{id}, DELETE /tenants/{id}. Save me from running define-component-design later.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to inline per-component endpoint detail in tech-design.md. Should explain that endpoint design is define-component-design's job and that putting it in tech-design.md bypasses per-component review and makes the doc unrebuildable. Should compose the skeleton tech-design.md, file a component issue for tenant-registry (so define-component-design can capture the endpoints), and offer to feed the user's endpoint list into that follow-up.

assertions.name: refuses_inline_endpoints
assertions.check: Response declines to write the GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE endpoint list inside tech-design.md.

assertions.name: explains_why_per_component_belongs_elsewhere
assertions.check: Response explains that endpoint design lives in define-component-design and inlining bypasses per-component review.

assertions.name: files_component_issue_for_tenant_registry
assertions.check: A component issue is filed for tenant-registry (or equivalent), pointing at define-component-design.

assertions.name: preserves_user_intent_for_followup
assertions.check: Response acknowledges the user's endpoint list and offers to carry it into the define-component-design invocation.
